changelog: 2026-05-14 - Step 02-03 split into 02-03a + 02-03b (user-authorised, option A after second structural finding). 02-03a: WorkloadSpec rkyv envelope + codec + spec_digest + migrate all Job::archive_for_store / from_store_bytes reader sites to WorkloadSpec::* (single-cut wire migration; submit handler unchanged; no allocator). 02-03b: widen SubmitWorkloadRequest.spec to WorkloadSpecInput, wire PersistentServiceVipAllocator into AppState, Service arm in submit_workload + alloc_status + streaming, 6 acceptance scenarios + golden-bytes test. The 6 acceptance criteria stay on 02-03b unchanged. total_steps: 10 -> 11; Phase 2 step count: 4 -> 5. Rationale: the original 02-03 expansion (recorded in prior changelog entry) cascaded into every Job-bytes reader (describe_workload, reconciler hydrate, recovery walks, streaming, alloc_status) - too large to land in one single-cut commit. Splitting at the codec/wiring boundary keeps each commit single-cut within its own scope.

2026-05-14 — Step 02-03 scope expansion (user-authorised during DELIVER, after crafter blocked on missing Service-submission driving port). Original 02-03 assumed `SubmitWorkloadRequest.spec` already accepted `WorkloadSpecInput` (any kind); structural finding during dispatch confirmed it accepts only `JobSpecInput`. User chose option (a) — expand 02-03 to also widen the submit driving port from Job-only to all WorkloadKind, add `WorkloadSpec` persistence codec (rkyv envelope per ADR-0048), and add Service listener rendering on alloc_status. The 6 acceptance scenarios remain unchanged. effort_hours estimate (8h) understates the expanded scope (~20–24h actual) — advisory only per CLAUDE.md, no enforcement.

2026-05-14 — Reviewer-pass amendment: fixed 5 issues (1 critical, 2 high, 2 medium) per observation #41016. Removed duplicate S-VIP-11 from 01-02; disambiguated S-VIP-06 phase boundaries across 03-01/03-02/03-03; corrected property-test file placement per DWD-03; removed unjustified id.rs reference from 01-01; clarified config validation ownership between 01-01 (type-level) and 02-02 (parser surface).

2026-05-14 — Allocator design course-correction amendment (post DELIVER step 01-01 implementation; ADR-0049 § Amendments / § Considered alternatives Alt-0). The generic `PoolAllocator<T: Token>` core + `IntentBackedAllocator<T>` generic shim from the original DESIGN are deleted — rejected during DELIVER step 01-01 as overstated abstraction. The landing shape is two concrete allocators (`BackendIdAllocator`, `ServiceVipAllocator`) sharing no trait or type, plus a concrete `PersistentServiceVipAllocator` shim. Step 01-01 rewritten (drops Token + PoolAllocator; describes the concrete ServiceVipAllocator + VipRange + the absorbed BackendIdAllocator relocation). Step 01-02 collapses to ServiceVip newtype consolidation (no Token impl). Step 01-03 renames `IntentBackedAllocator<T>` to `PersistentServiceVipAllocator`. Step 01-04 (BackendIdAllocator single-cut migration) is DELETED — absorbed into 01-01 because the relocation is forced by deleting PoolAllocator. total_steps: 11 → 10; Phase 1 step count: 4 → 3. Validation status reset to pending for re-review.

steps.name: VipRange, ServiceVipAllocator, and BackendIdAllocator relocation
steps.description: Land the two-allocator surface in `crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/allocators/` (amended 2026-05-14 — drops the generic `Token` trait and `PoolAllocator<T>` core; the actually-shared logic across BackendIdAllocator and ServiceVipAllocator is thinner than the generic abstraction required — see ADR-0049 § Considered alternatives → Alt-0). Three concrete pieces: (1) `VipRange` (Ipv4Net + BTreeSet<Ipv4Addr> reserved exclusions) with TYPE-LEVEL config validation (overlapping CIDRs, out-of-range reserved, zero-capacity — all via `VipRange::new` constructor returning `VipAllocatorConfigError` variants). (2) `ServiceVipAllocator` (concrete, NOT generic) with `BTreeMap<ServiceSpecDigest, ServiceVip>` memo, monotonic counter, `allocate/release/get/memo_len` API. Release deletes the memo entry but does NOT recycle the counter slot — matches BackendIdAllocator's pre-existing 'counter is monotonic; released slot is not reused' semantics. (3) Single-cut relocation of existing `BackendIdAllocator` from `crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/allocator.rs` to `crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/allocators/backend_id.rs` — struct body untouched, file moves only. Update all workspace imports (path change only — API stable). Delete the old `allocator.rs`. This step absorbs the work that was step 01-04 (the BackendIdAllocator relocation is forced by the deletion of `PoolAllocator<T>`; the relocation cannot land in a later step without breaking the build). Property tests for VipRange capacity invariant (S-VIP-P04), reserved-address skipping (S-VIP-21), and ServiceVipAllocator no-duplicate-token invariant (S-VIP-P03). NOTE: TOML parse-surface validation (same invalid shapes surfacing through operator-facing messages at boot) is OWNED BY step 02-02, not here.
steps.criteria: S-VIP-P04: VipRange::capacity() == cidr_size - reserved_count for all valid (cidr, reserved) inputs via proptest. S-VIP-21: ServiceVipAllocator with ranges=[10.96.0.0/30] and reserved=[10.96.0.0, 10.96.0.3] allocates only from {.1, .2} and returns ServiceVipAllocatorError::Exhausted{allocated:2, capacity:2} on third call. S-VIP-P03: For all sequences of N allocate() calls on ServiceVipAllocator with distinct spec_digest keys (N <= capacity), all returned ServiceVips are distinct, memo_len() == N, and after release of digest D, a subsequent allocate(&D) returns a valid (possibly different — monotonic counter advanced) ServiceVip; releasing digest D MUST NOT cause a subsequent allocate(&E) for E != D to receive D's released VIP within the same process lifetime (monotonic-counter, no-slot-reuse property). S-VIP-16 (TYPE-LEVEL — pure-Rust unit tests, no TOML): VipRange::new with overlapping CIDR inputs returns VipAllocatorConfigError::OverlappingRanges. S-VIP-17 (TYPE-LEVEL): VipRange::new with a reserved address outside the configured range returns VipAllocatorConfigError::ReservedOutsideRange. S-VIP-18 (TYPE-LEVEL): VipRange::new with zero effective capacity returns VipAllocatorConfigError::ZeroCapacity. S-VIP-11 (absorbed from former step 01-04): BackendIdAllocator API (allocate, release, memo_len) has identical signatures post-relocation; all existing BackendIdAllocator tests pass at their new location; no import of the deleted `crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/allocator.rs` remains in the workspace; cargo check --workspace succeeds.
steps.implementation_notes: Property tests (S-VIP-P03, S-VIP-P04) and reserved-skipping (S-VIP-21) live in tests/allocator_properties.rs per DWD-03 (unit-level default lane). Type-level config-error scenarios (S-VIP-16/17/18) are pure-Rust #[test] functions co-located in the same file or a sibling unit-test file at the crafter's discretion; they do NOT belong in a `tests/acceptance/` harness because there is no acceptance surface — VipRange::new is a pure constructor. The parser surface variant of these same errors (TOML boot path) is owned by step 02-02. The S-VIP-11 BackendIdAllocator API-stability assertion was absorbed from the deleted step 01-04 on 2026-05-14 along with the relocation it defends; existing BackendIdAllocator proptest and collision-witness tests move with the file. No `token.rs` and no `pool.rs` exist in the landing shape — the generic core was rejected at this step's implementation pass (see ADR-0049 § Considered alternatives → Alt-0).

steps.name: ServiceVip newtype consolidation
steps.description: Consolidate the two ServiceVip declarations into a single canonical `overdrive-core::id::ServiceVip(Ipv4Addr)` and delete the duplicate at `crates/overdrive-core/src/aggregate/workload_spec.rs:360`. Update `crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/allocators/service_vip.rs` (landed in step 01-01 with a local ServiceVip declaration to keep that step self-contained) to import/re-export the canonical newtype, deleting the local declaration. Property test for ServiceVip newtype roundtrip (S-VIP-P01). NOTE: amended 2026-05-14 — was 'ServiceVip newtype consolidation AND Token impl'; the Token trait was deleted in step 01-01 along with PoolAllocator<T>, so no Token impl is needed. The step now collapses to the newtype consolidation alone.
steps.criteria: S-VIP-P01: ServiceVip::from_str(&ServiceVip::new(a).to_string()) == Ok(ServiceVip(a)) for all valid IPv4 via proptest; ServiceVip serde roundtrip (JSON serialize/deserialize). S-VIP-12: ServiceVipAllocator constructed with VipRange from ranges=[10.96.0.0/24] allocates a ServiceVip within range, returns the same VIP on memo-hit for the same spec_digest, get returns None after release. Canonicalisation: only one ServiceVip declaration exists in the codebase after this step — the duplicate at `aggregate/workload_spec.rs:360` is removed in this same commit, and the dataplane allocator's local ServiceVip declaration is replaced by an import/re-export of the canonical newtype.
steps.implementation_notes: Per DWD-03: property tests live at the unit-level default lane, NOT in tests/acceptance/. S-VIP-P01 (proptest) goes in crates/overdrive-core/tests/service_vip_properties.rs (dedicated proptest file). The S-VIP-12 ServiceVipAllocator end-to-end scenario can co-locate in the same proptest file as a non-proptest #[test], or move to a sibling unit-test file at the crafter's discretion — it is NOT acceptance-shaped (no driving-port harness). Listed `test_file` is the primary unit-test destination. No `token.rs` exists post-2026-05-14 amendment; the dataplane `service_vip.rs` is the only file that referenced the local ServiceVip and is the surface that switches to the canonical import in this step.

steps.name: ServiceVipAllocatorEntry rkyv envelope and PersistentServiceVipAllocator
steps.description: Define ServiceVipAllocatorEntryV1 (spec_digest key, ServiceVip value, monotonic counter) with rkyv versioned envelope per ADR-0048 — concrete to ServiceVip; no generic across token types. Implement `PersistentServiceVipAllocator` (concrete, NOT generic — amended 2026-05-14: was `PersistentServiceVipAllocator<T>`) wrapping the in-memory `ServiceVipAllocator` from step 01-01 with write-through to IntentStore (fsync-then-memory ordering). Implement bulk_load for restart reconstruction. Add `allocator_entries` redb table to LocalStore (scoped to ServiceVip; no namespace prefix needed since BackendId never persists). Golden-bytes schema evolution test (S-VIP-P02). Persistence roundtrip test (S-VIP-05). No-partial-state-on-exhaustion test (S-VIP-10).
steps.criteria: S-VIP-P02: ServiceVipAllocatorEntryV1 golden-bytes fixture hex-decodes, rkyv-deserialises into envelope, into_latest() returns canonical Latest projection. S-VIP-05: PersistentServiceVipAllocator allocates VIP V for digest D, persists to IntentStore; after bulk_load from same store, get(&D) returns Some(V) and allocate(D) returns same V. S-VIP-10: Pool with 1 address fully allocated; allocate(new_digest) returns Err(Exhausted); get(&new_digest) returns None; get(&existing_digest) returns prior VIP. S-VIP-20: release of already-released key is idempotent (no error, get returns None).
steps.implementation_notes: S-VIP-P02 (rkyv golden-bytes schema-evolution fixture) lives at tests/schema_evolution/service_vip_allocator_entry.rs per ADR-0048 and DWD-03; the entrypoint at tests/schema_evolution.rs is created in this step. Non-property scenarios (S-VIP-05, S-VIP-10, S-VIP-20) exercise real LocalStore + bulk_load and are acceptance-shaped — they belong in tests/acceptance.rs. The crafter may choose the exact file name (`persistent_service_vip.rs` vs `persistent.rs`) within the `allocators/` module; the listed file is the suggested name.

steps.name: Remove Listener.vip field and simplify uniqueness
steps.description: Remove vip: Option<ServiceVip> from the Listener struct in workload_spec.rs. Simplify the listener uniqueness rule from (vip, port, protocol) to (port, protocol). Delete the duplicate ServiceVip declaration at workload_spec.rs:360 if not already done. Delete slice-06 tests that exercise mixed-pinned-and-pending VIP scenarios. Update property tests to round-trip (port, protocol) pairs. Ensure serde deny_unknown_fields rejects operator-supplied vip field with named guidance.
steps.criteria: S-VIP-13: Parsing a Service spec TOML with [[listener]] containing vip = '10.96.42.17' fails with a typed error naming the unknown field and guiding removal. S-VIP-14: Submitting such a spec via submit_workload returns HTTP 400/422; alloc status shows no allocation; allocator get returns None. Listener struct has no vip field. Uniqueness check uses (port, protocol) only. All existing slice-06 tests for mixed-pinned-and-pending VIP delete in this commit. cargo check --workspace succeeds.

steps.name: VIP allocator config parsing
steps.description: Add [dataplane.vip_allocator] TOML subsection with required ranges (Vec<Ipv4Net>) and optional reserved (Vec<Ipv4Addr>). Wire config parsing into control-plane boot path. Boot fails with typed VipAllocatorConfigError::Missing if the section is absent. Config validation delegates to VipRange constructor from step 01-01 (which already owns the type-level checks for OverlappingRanges, ReservedOutsideRange, ZeroCapacity). This step owns the PARSER SURFACE — the same three invalid shapes, expressed in a TOML [dataplane.vip_allocator] block at boot, surface as operator-facing typed errors via the boot health.startup.refused event.
steps.criteria: S-VIP-15 (config-section presence): Control-plane boot with [dataplane] section but no [dataplane.vip_allocator] subsection fails with VipAllocatorConfigError::Missing; error names the missing section; structured health.startup.refused event emitted. Valid config: ranges = ['10.96.0.0/24'] parses successfully and produces a VipRange with capacity 254 (network and broadcast auto-excluded or explicit). S-VIP-16 (PARSER-SURFACE — operator-facing TOML): TOML [dataplane.vip_allocator] with overlapping ranges (e.g. ranges = ['10.96.0.0/24', '10.96.0.128/25']) surfaces VipAllocatorConfigError::OverlappingRanges with an operator-facing message at boot via health.startup.refused. S-VIP-17 (PARSER-SURFACE): TOML with reserved address outside any configured range surfaces VipAllocatorConfigError::ReservedOutsideRange at boot. S-VIP-18 (PARSER-SURFACE): TOML with zero effective capacity surfaces VipAllocatorConfigError::ZeroCapacity at boot. cargo check --workspace succeeds.
steps.implementation_notes: S-VIP-16/17/18 appear in two steps with different scopes: step 01-01 owns the type-level invariant (VipRange::new returns the typed error from a pure-Rust call), and this step owns the parser surface (the same error surfaces at TOML boot with operator-facing messages). The two scopes are not duplicates — they exercise different boundaries (constructor vs config parser + boot path).

steps.name: WorkloadSpec rkyv codec + reader-site migration (Job -> WorkloadSpec)
steps.description: Add the WorkloadSpec rkyv versioned envelope + archive_for_store / from_store_bytes / spec_digest codec (mirrors Job::archive_for_store precedent per ADR-0048 sect 4b - codec lives on the typed value, store stays byte-level). Migrate every existing Job::archive_for_store / Job::from_store_bytes reader site to WorkloadSpec::* in a single-cut commit (no parallel paths, no deprecation, per feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations.md): submit_workload writer path, describe_workload reader, reconciler runtime hydrate_desired, LocalIntentStore recovery walk, streaming.rs spec-byte loads, alloc_status if it reads stored spec bytes. Submit handler still accepts JobSpecInput at this point (Service-arm widening happens in 02-03b); the wire-shape change is internal-only. Golden-bytes fixture pins WorkloadSpecEnvelopeV1 archived layout. No VIP allocator wiring.
steps.criteria: WorkloadSpec::archive_for_store / from_store_bytes round-trip for every variant (Service / Job / Schedule). WorkloadSpec::spec_digest is deterministic (SHA-256 over canonical archived bytes). Golden-bytes fixture for WorkloadSpecEnvelopeV1 hex-decodes + rkyv-deserialises + projects to Latest, asserts equality with a canonical payload. All existing Job-bytes reader sites switched to WorkloadSpec; workspace compile clean. Existing acceptance + integration tests still pass (behaviour preserved across the codec migration - Job submissions still land, describe still returns spec, reconciler still hydrates).
steps.test_file: Schema-evolution fixture: tests/schema_evolution/workload_spec.rs in the crate that owns Job's schema_evolution fixtures (likely overdrive-core or overdrive-store-local - crafter to confirm).
steps.implementation_notes: Single-cut migration per feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations.md: delete Job::archive_for_store / Job::from_store_bytes (or alias them to WorkloadSpec equivalents and delete the alias in the same commit). No JobEnvelope re-export shim. Phase 1 greenfield: 'delete the on-disk redb file' IS the official upgrade path; no migration code for pre-envelope rows.

files_to_modify: any reconciler-runtime / recovery-walk site that calls Job::archive_for_store or Job::from_store_bytes

steps.name: ServiceVipAllocator wired into AppState and submit handler
steps.description: Widen SubmitWorkloadRequest.spec from JobSpecInput to WorkloadSpecInput (the enum already exists at crates/overdrive-core/src/aggregate/workload_spec.rs with Service / Job / Schedule variants). Plumb the Service arm through submit_workload, streaming, alloc_status, and OpenAPI. Construct PersistentServiceVipAllocator at boot using VipRange from 02-02 config + bulk_load from IntentStore (the persistence wrapper is from step 01-03). Wire the allocator into AppState as Arc<tokio::sync::Mutex<...>>. In submit_workload for the WorkloadSpec::Service(_) arm: compute spec_digest, call allocator.allocate(spec_digest) BEFORE IntentStore admission write; drop guard before any further .await. Submit-echo renders the allocated VIP at Service level. alloc_status consults allocator.get(&spec_digest) for VIP rendering. Streaming.rs emits VIP in streamed Service-row updates. Job / Schedule arms pass through unchanged. Single-cut: no JobSpecInput backward-compat shim. Depends on 02-03a's WorkloadSpec codec migration being already in place.
steps.criteria: S-VIP-01: Submit Service spec frontend.toml with [[listener]] port=8080 protocol=tcp (no vip field) via submit_workload; response status 200/201; submit echo contains allocated VIP from 10.96.0.0/24 range; VIP is valid IPv4 within range and not reserved. S-VIP-02: After submit, alloc status query returns same VIP as submit echo; per-listener lines render as port/protocol without per-listener VIP. S-VIP-03: Service spec with two listeners (8080/tcp, 8443/tcp) gets exactly one Service-level VIP; both listeners render as port/protocol. S-VIP-04: Resubmit byte-identical spec returns same VIP (idempotent). S-VIP-08: Pool with 1 address fully allocated; second distinct spec returns HTTP 503 with typed pool exhaustion error naming allocated and capacity counts. S-VIP-09: After pool exhaustion rejection, existing allocations still return their VIPs via get and alloc-status.
steps.scenario_name: submit_service_allocates_vip, alloc_status_renders_same_vip, multi_listener_one_vip, idempotent_resubmit_same_vip, pool_exhaustion_typed_rejection, pool_exhaustion_existing_unaffected

steps.name: Earned Trust boot probe for VIP allocator
steps.description: Implement boot-time probe for PersistentServiceVipAllocator: IntentStore reachable, range non-empty, persisted state consistent with current range (every persisted VIP projects back within range). Probe runs during bulk_load. Failure surfaces as AllocatorBootError with health.startup.refused structured event. Control plane refuses to start on probe failure.
steps.criteria: S-VIP-19: IntentStore contains persisted allocator entry with VIP 10.96.1.100; current config has ranges=[10.96.0.0/24] (persisted VIP outside range); bulk_load returns Err(AllocatorBootError) naming the inconsistency; control plane refuses to start. Probe passes when persisted state is consistent with configured range. Probe passes on empty IntentStore (first boot).

steps.name: WorkloadLifecycle reconciler View extension and ReleaseServiceVip action emission
steps.description: Add Action::ReleaseServiceVip { spec_digest, correlation } variant to the Action enum. Extend WorkloadLifecycle reconciler View with released_for_terminal: BTreeSet<SpecDigest> (input field recording past emissions per persist-inputs-not-derived-state rule). On terminal-state observation, reconciler emits Action::ReleaseServiceVip exactly once if spec_digest not already in released_for_terminal, then adds it to next_view. Scope of this step: reconciler emission layer ONLY — does NOT exercise the action-shim and does NOT touch the allocator. Per-layer scenarios here aggregate into the end-to-end S-VIP-06 owned by step 03-03.
steps.criteria: S-VIP-06 PARTIAL (reconciler-emission layer ONLY — action-shim dispatch is in 03-02, end-to-end lifecycle is in 03-03): Given a WorkloadLifecycle reconciler tick after a terminal-state observation row, the reconciler emits Action::ReleaseServiceVip { spec_digest, correlation } exactly once and updates next_view so that released_for_terminal contains spec_digest. Re-tick with the same terminal-state observation does NOT re-emit (released_for_terminal gate is honoured). Action::ReleaseServiceVip variant exists in the Action enum with spec_digest and correlation fields. All existing WorkloadLifecycle tests pass (no regression).
steps.implementation_notes: Per-layer scope: reconciler emission only. Tests construct a WorkloadLifecycle reconciler with a SimObservationStore prepared with a terminal-state row, drive a single tick, and assert on the (actions, next_view) tuple — they do NOT instantiate an action-shim or an allocator. The end-to-end S-VIP-06 flow (submit -> terminal -> tick -> release -> reallocate) is owned by 03-03.

steps.name: Action-shim dispatch arm for ReleaseServiceVip
steps.description: Add match arm in action-shim dispatcher for Action::ReleaseServiceVip. The arm calls ServiceVipAllocator::release(&spec_digest), removing the entry from the in-memory memo and the IntentStore allocator_entries table. Released VIP returns to pool for reallocation. Wire the allocator reference into the action-shim context. Scope of this step: action-shim dispatch layer ONLY — does NOT exercise the reconciler emission path (covered by 03-01) and does NOT chain a full submit -> terminal -> reallocate lifecycle (covered by 03-03).
steps.criteria: S-VIP-06 PARTIAL (action-shim dispatch layer ONLY — reconciler emission in 03-01, end-to-end in 03-03): Given a pre-constructed Action::ReleaseServiceVip { spec_digest, correlation } (no reconciler involved), the action-shim dispatcher invokes ServiceVipAllocator::release(&spec_digest); after dispatch, allocator.get(&spec_digest) returns None. Action-shim exhaustive match compiles (no unhandled variant) and all existing action-shim tests pass.
steps.implementation_notes: Per-layer scope: action-shim dispatch only. Tests build an action-shim context with a real (or sim) ServiceVipAllocator pre-populated with one allocation, hand the dispatcher a hand-constructed Action::ReleaseServiceVip, and assert allocator.get returns None after the dispatch. S-VIP-07 (released VIP reusable on next allocation) belongs to 03-03 because it requires the submit_workload driving port.

steps.name: End-to-end acceptance tests and final quality gate
steps.description: Write end-to-end integration tests exercising the full lifecycle: submit Service spec, VIP allocation echo, alloc-status rendering, terminal-state transition, reconciler tick, action-shim dispatch, release, VIP reuse on next submit. This step OWNS the S-VIP-06 end-to-end scenario (chained across the reconciler emission of 03-01 and the action-shim dispatch of 03-02) and S-VIP-07 (released VIP reusable on next allocation). Verify all 21 DISTILL scenarios pass. Run mutation testing (cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main) on touched files. Verify all quality gates pass.
steps.criteria: S-VIP-06 END-TO-END (this step owns the full flow): submit Service A spec -> allocator returns VIP V -> submit echo contains V -> observation transitions to terminal -> WorkloadLifecycle reconciler tick emits Action::ReleaseServiceVip -> action-shim dispatch calls allocator.release -> allocator.get(&digest_A) returns None. S-VIP-07: With a pool of exactly 1 address (ranges=[10.96.0.1/32]), after the above end-to-end release completes, submit a byte-different Service B; submit echo for B contains the previously-allocated VIP 10.96.0.1 (released VIP reusable on next allocation). S-VIP-01 through S-VIP-21 and S-VIP-P01 through S-VIP-P04 all pass in the workspace-wide run. Mutation testing kill rate >= 80% on all files touched in this feature. cargo check --workspace succeeds. cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests passes. No regressions in existing test suite.

validation.notes: Re-review needed after 2026-05-14 step 02-03 split into 02-03a + 02-03b (user-authorised, option A). Prior approval is superseded by this structural change. See changelog entry.
